title: sterculia corrugata
intro: Sterculia corrugata, also known as the wright's sterculia, is a species of tree in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. In traditional herbal preparations, Sterculia corrugata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
